title: IncidentReport
description: Canonical shape consumed by Container 1 (Anomaly Intake) for a humanoid RL training fleet. Every source — live reward/loss telemetry, container training logs, a hardware monitor (GPU/thermal/quota), or a researcher console — normalizes its input to this shape before entering the BPMN. The Telemetry Analyst agent, the Researcher Review Board Action Center HITL, and downstream containers all read this object. One record = one training-run anomaly/event.

incidentId.description: Stable identifier assigned by the source adapter. Format: `IR-{YYYYMMDD}-{seq}` recommended (IR = Incident Report / run event).

type.description: Source category. Determines which adapter normalized the input. `telemetry`=live reward/loss metric stream, `logs`=container/training stdout logs, `monitor`=hardware monitor (GPU utilization/thermal/quota/billing), `manual`=researcher console/form.

detectorConfidence.description: When applicable (automated detectors), the source's confidence score. Used by the Risk Agent's Fast Track gateway. Omit or set to 1.0 for manual/deterministic sources.

occurredAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the underlying event occurred (not when the trigger fired the BPMN).

severity.description: Initial severity tag from the source. The Risk Agent may revise this in Container 3.

subject.description: Short human-readable headline (e.g., `LOSS_DIVERGENCE: critic loss diverging on run walk-gait-v2`).

description.description: Free-form details supplied by the source. May include error codes, observed measurements (reward, loss, gradient norm, GPU temperature), comparison to a healthy baseline, attached evidence URIs.

affectedItems.description: Training jobs / GPU instances / cloud accounts implicated by the anomaly. Drives cloud-provider & cost impact analysis in Container 4.

itemId.description: Training job id, GPU instance id, cluster id, or cloud account id.

supplierId.description: Optional cloud-provider / account identifier; populated when a resource is sourced from a specific provider.

quantity.description: Affected quantity or count of instances/jobs in the affected batch.

safetyZone.description: Criticality zone of the run when the anomaly occurred. Critical zones (high-cost-gpu, production-bound, public-benchmark, long-running) bias the Risk Agent toward Full Track / human review — wasted spend, a model headed for a physical robot, a published benchmark, or a multi-day run all raise the stakes of a wrong autonomous call.

evidence.description: Supporting evidence URIs (reward-curve captures, log excerpts, GPU dashboards).

uri.description: Cloud storage or content-addressable URI to the evidence artifact.

reportedBy.description: Human or system that filed the event (the `Requestor` role in the framework).

principalId.description: User ID, service account name, or detector instance ID.

tags.description: Free-form tags used by the Analyst agent for clustering related events.

vendor.description: Source-specific raw payload, preserved verbatim for audit. Adapters MUST NOT mutate; downstream agents may consult.
